title: API Authentication Guide
bodyMarkdown:
How callers authenticate against the Helios serving API.

## Token model
Requests carry a signed bearer token issued by src/api/auth.ts. Tokens encode a subject, a list of scopes, and an expiry, and are verified on every request before it reaches the router. There is no session state on the server beyond the token itself.

## Requesting a token
Service accounts get long-lived tokens issued during provisioning. Human callers, mostly for debugging against staging, request a short-lived token through the internal tooling, default expiry one hour.

## Scopes
The common scope is serve:invoke, required for any inference request. An admin scope exists for internal tooling and bypasses individual scope checks; it is not handed out to service accounts.

## Failure modes
An expired token or a bad signature both fail closed with a rejected request, no partial trust. If your token is being rejected unexpectedly, check the expiry first, that is the most common cause.

## Owner
Owen Reyes owns the API and auth surface. File requests for new scopes with him.
